message:
test(pty): adversarial deserialize fuzz for CommandBuilder

Adds frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_deserialize_fuzz.rs —
a structure-aware fuzz harness that attacks the CommandBuilder serde
adapter from the receive side, complementing the happy-path round-trip
suite in proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs.

Metamorphic robustness relation:

    ∀ bytes ∈ Vec<u8>.  deserialize(bytes) ∈ Ok | Err — never panic.

A panic on the deserialize path is a denial-of-service against any
process that consumes serialized CommandBuilders (the mux server's
SpawnV2 PDU, the test CAS over MCP, the workflow runner's
per-invocation spawn plumbing). All of those paths eventually route
through `serde_json::from_value::<CommandBuilder>` on bytes from an
untrusted sender.

Four properties, 1024 cases each (~4k total fuzz iterations):

  (1) `deserialize_random_bytes_never_panics` — pure random Vec<u8>
      of length 0..=1024 through `serde_json::from_slice`. Covers the
      lex / parse edge entirely.

  (2) `deserialize_random_json_never_panics` — random
      `serde_json::Value` shapes recursively nested up to depth 3
      (null, bool, number, string, array, object). This stresses the
      structural-validator layer in `env_map_serde::deserialize`
      that the byte-level fuzz rarely reaches.

  (3) `deserialize_mismatched_env_key_is_rejected_cleanly` —
      hand-crafts a plausible CommandBuilder JSON frame, then forces
      an env pair's outer key to diverge from the inner
      `preferred_key`'s `map_key` normalization. Confirms the strict
      mismatch guard landed in 47e5c138 fires as a typed error
      ("does not match preferred_key" / "invalid" / "expected"),
      never a panic. Unix-gated because on Windows `map_key` is a
      no-op so the mismatch fixture can't force a divergence.

  (4) `deserialize_duplicate_env_entry_is_rejected_cleanly` — same
      adversarial frame but clones the last env entry to create a
      duplicate. Confirms the duplicate guard from 47e5c138 produces
      a typed "duplicate" error.

Verified: `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/pty/Cargo.toml
--test proptest_cmdbuilder_deserialize_fuzz` — `test result: ok. 4
passed; 0 failed` in 0.42s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target after a 37.91s
cold compile.

Harness ran clean on first pass — no regressions surfaced on any of
the 4,096 generated inputs, confirming the deserialize pipeline
(the parser lexer, the env_map_serde validator, and the outer
CommandBuilder derive) is robust against the edges this harness
explores.
